hero.desc: Not in perfect lighting, but in real life. Gentle, precise, and thoughtfully planned for Malaysian skin.

realityCheck.title: Because your skin is not a filter
realityCheck.desc: If you're dealing with pigment that keeps coming back, acne marks that refuse to fade, or a "why do I look tired" tone even after skincare, Pico Laser can be a smart next step.
realityCheck.highlight: It's not magic. It's medical precision.

whatIs.title: What is Pico Laser?
whatIs.desc: Pico Laser delivers energy in ultra short bursts—faster than a snap of your fingers. It breaks pigment into tiny particles and stimulates skin remodeling while limiting excess heat spread.
whatIs.point2: Minimizes heat damage to surrounding skin
whatIs.point3: Safe for Malaysian skin tones when done correctly

whyMalaysian.title: For Malaysian skin tones
whyMalaysian.desc: Many Malaysians sit in medium to deeper skin tone ranges. Heat-based treatments can trigger pigmentation issues—Pico Laser's ultra short pulses minimize this risk.
whyMalaysian.safeBadge: Recognized safe for darker skin tones
whyMalaysian.quote: "Picosecond lasers minimize unwanted heat damage while maintaining efficacy"
whyMalaysian.quoteSource: — Clinical Review on Treating PIH in Skin of Colour

approach.title: How we keep it safe and worth it
approach.step1Desc: We analyze your pigment type, inflammation level, and past reactions
approach.step2Desc: Essential for reactive skin or history of PIH
approach.step3Desc: Laser without aftercare is like gym without protein

sessions.title: How many sessions?
sessions.desc: Based on your concern and how your skin responds:
sessions.sessions4: Depends on ink colour and depth
sessions.downtimeDesc: Most people don't need to hide at home. Mild redness for a few hours—that's it.

prosCons.title: Honest pros & cons
prosCons.pro1: Targets pigment with less heat spread
prosCons.pro3: Improves tone and texture over time
prosCons.con4: Deep scars may need combination treatments

faq.a1: Most people feel quick snaps or mild tingling—very tolerable.
faq.a2: Mild peeling can happen, especially when targeting pigmentation.
faq.q3: Can I wash my face after?
faq.a3: Yes, gently with lukewarm water and mild cleanser.
faq.q4: Is it safe for darker skin?
faq.a4: Yes, when performed correctly with proper settings.
faq.a5: Melasma can improve but recurrence is common—we plan long-term.

cta.title: Start with a proper diagnosis
cta.desc: Book an assessment at Nexus Clinic Kuala Lumpur. Choose the right settings and schedule for your skin.
cta.address: LG 10, Lower Ground Floor, Wisma UOA II, Jalan Pinang, 50450 Kuala Lumpur
